_comment: Phase 1 §1.D semantic-golden fixture for Form 135 — REGENERATED after the BiDi-correct hebrewForPdf rewrite. Each `stampedPerPage[i]` is the ORDERED list of stamped values pdf-parse emits at the tail of page (i+1), in stream order. Hebrew strings now appear in LOGICAL Unicode order (e.g. "אוהד", "בר", "תל", "אביב", "דיזנגוף", "כן") because lib/bidi.ts:shapeForPdf() emits logical-order text with an RTL_MARK prefix (the prefix is invisible to pdf-parse's tokenizer). Pre-Phase-1 the fixture had reversed glyph sequences ("דהוא", "רב", "ביבא", "לת", "ףוגנזיד", "ןכ") because hebrewForPdf() did `text.split("").reverse()`. The brief retired that reversal so SHAAM intake OCR can read Hebrew names off the PDF text stream — see audits/generation.md §1.5 + §1.9. `requiredCodes` is the set of 3-digit codes the form template MUST still print; a scripts/build-field-map.mjs denylist regression that drops one of these surfaces here.
